info.title: Sales CoPilot API
info.description:
REST API for Sales CoPilot — an AI-powered presentation platform with narrated slides, live Q&A, and deck management.

**Authentication**: Most endpoints require a session cookie obtained from `POST /api/auth/login`. Presentation playback endpoints (`/api/qa`, `/api/narration/*`) also accept a `presentationId` for unauthenticated viewer access.

tags.name: Q&A
tags.description: Live question answering and Q&A settings

tags.name: Presentations
tags.description: Create and manage personalized presentation instances

tags.name: Generation
tags.description: AI-powered content generation from slide images

tags.name: Company
tags.description: Organization contact info and knowledge base

tags.name: Roof Measurements
tags.description: Request and track automated roof measurements

tags.name: Actions
tags.description: Voice/text command detection for presentation control

presentationToken.name: x-presentation-token
presentationToken.description: HMAC-signed token scoped to a specific presentation. Obtained from `POST /api/presentations/{presentationId}/events-token`.

deckContext.description: Background info used to guide AI narration generation

previewImageUrl.description: Presigned URL for the first slide image

audio_url.description: S3 key for the slide audio file

hero_img.description: S3 key for the slide image

userMessage.description: Background color for homeowner chat message bubbles. Falls back to primary color if not set.

assistantMessage.description: Background color for AI assistant chat message bubbles.

avatar.description: S3 key for rep avatar image

audioUrl.description: S3 key for the pre-generated answer audio

resumePromptText.description: Spoken prompt asking if the viewer wants to continue the presentation

transitionPromptText.description: Spoken bridge from Q&A back into the presentation

audioUrl.description: Accessible URL for the synthesized audio
Accessible URL for the TTS audio of the answer

backgroundImage.description: S3 key or URL for intro background image

channelConfig.description: Channel-specific configuration (e.g. URL for webhook, toEmails for email)

ProductSlideData.description: Good/better/best product pricing tiers for a product pricing slide.

get.description: Returns all decks for the authenticated organization, ordered by most recently created.

deckContext.description: Background information to guide AI narration generation (e.g. audience, purpose)

get.description: Returns the full deck including slides, FAQs, branding, and Q&A settings.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a deck and all associated slides, FAQs, and branding.

slideIds.description: Full ordered array of slide UUIDs. Position in array becomes the new orderIndex.

post.description: Creates a FAQ and automatically generates TTS audio for the answer.

warning.description: Present if audio generation failed (FAQ is still saved)

200.description: Branding configuration, or null if not yet set

put.description: Creates or replaces the branding configuration for a deck.

avatar.description: S3 key for rep avatar image

get.description: Returns the customizable resume and transition prompts for the deck's Q&A experience.

post.description: Accepts uploaded slide images, runs them through GPT-4o Vision to extract content, generates narration scripts, and synthesizes audio. Returns a job ID — poll `/api/decks/{deckId}/generate/status` for progress.

images.description: Slide images (PNG, JPG, or WebP; max 10 MB each)

get.description: Returns all non-deleted presentations created from this deck.

post.description:
Creates a personalized presentation for a homeowner. The address is validated via Google Geocoding. Returns a shareable URL immediately; audio generation may continue in the background.

Price inputs are optional. If a product pricing slide uses formula variables that are not supplied, the price is deferred — the slide displays a placeholder instead of failing. Deferred prices can be resolved later when the missing values become available (e.g., after roof measurements).

price_inputs.description: Values for deck-level price variables (used to evaluate formulas). Optional — omitted variables result in deferred pricing rather than an error.

patch.description: Partial update for homeowner data, voice settings, language, or price inputs.

delete.description: Soft-deletes a presentation (sets `deletedAt`). The shareable URL stops working immediately.

get.description: Returns the current processing status and slide readiness.

post.description:
Answers a viewer question. Checks FAQs first (exact then semantic match at >= 0.6 confidence), falls back to GPT-4o with slide context. Returns answer text plus synthesized audio.

**Authentication**: Accepts either a session cookie (admin) or a `presentationId` (public viewer).
Generates TTS audio for a slide script. Results are cached per session.
Batch TTS generation. Per-slide failures return a fallback audio URL rather than failing the whole batch.
Generates the spoken bridge from Q&A back into the presentation, using the deck's configured transition prompt.

get.description: Returns all uploaded images for the authenticated organization.

post.description: Uploads and optimizes an image (max 1920px width, JPEG quality 85). Returns the S3 key and a presigned URL.

file.description: Image file (PNG, JPG, or WebP; max 10 MB)

get.description: Returns a curated list of voices suitable for sales presentations.
Returns `{ ok: true }` if the service is running.

post.description: Authenticates with email and password. Returns user and organization info and sets a session cookie. Rate-limited to prevent brute-force attacks.

200.description: Login successful. A `session` cookie is set on the response.

post.description: Revokes the current session and clears the session cookie.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's identity and organization.

get.summary: Portal SSO via auth code exchange
get.description:
Exchanges a short-lived authorization code for an access token via the Core API, creates a new session, and redirects to the requested path. Used by the portal to embed Sales CoPilot in an iframe.

Flow:
1. Exchange `code` via Core API `POST /auth/code/exchange` (Basic auth with SSO client credentials)
2. Validate the returned access token via `/user/me` to get user/org info
3. Create a new session (always — never reuses an existing one)
4. Set session cookie (plus embedded cookie if `embedded=1`)
5. Revoke any prior session (best-effort)
6. Redirect to a clean URL (no `code` param in the final location)

Redirect URLs use the `APP_URL` environment variable as the canonical base origin when set. This is required for production deployments behind a load balancer where the internal hostname differs from the public URL.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: Short-lived authorization code issued by the portal

parameters.name: redirect
parameters.description: Internal path to redirect to after successful authentication

parameters.name: embedded
parameters.description: Set to `1` when running inside a portal iframe. Enables embedded mode cookies and error handling.

parameters.name: theme
parameters.description: Portal theme preference. When set, a theme cookie is stored so the initial render matches the portal appearance without a flash.

302.description: Redirects to the `redirect` path on success, or to `/login?error=sso_failed` (standalone) / `/embedded-auth-error` (embedded) on failure. Returns a redirect on missing or invalid code, or Core API rejection.

get.description: Returns the organization's contact details used in contracts and branding.
Returns the company's knowledge base profile that guides how the AI answers customer questions.

put.description: Updates the company tagline, about text, trust signals, service coverage, and answer style used by the AI.

get.description: Returns all uploaded knowledge base documents for the organization, with usage metadata.

post.description: Validates the file and returns a presigned S3 URL for direct upload. Call `/api/company/kb/documents/complete` after the upload finishes.

uploadUrl.description: Presigned S3 URL for PUT upload

400.description: Bad request (invalid file type, size, or document limit reached)

post.description: Marks a document as uploaded after the client finishes uploading to S3. Triggers background processing (text extraction, chunking, and embedding).

delete.description: Removes the document from S3 and the database. Associated chunks are deleted automatically.

post.description: Uploads a contract document (PDF or Word), extracts text, and uses AI to analyze it for template conversion. Returns detected variables that can be used in contract templates.

file.description: Contract file (PDF or DOCX, max 10 MB)

400.description: Bad request (unsupported file type or too large)

get.description: Returns all action configurations (contract templates) for a deck.

post.description: Creates a new contract template or action configuration for a deck.

get.description: Returns the intro page configuration for a deck, including the background image, headline, and CTA button label.

200.description: Intro configuration, or null if not set

put.description: Creates or updates the intro page for a deck. Enabling an intro page switches the deck to lazy generation mode.

backgroundImage.description: S3 key for the intro background image

get.description: Returns the good/better/best product pricing tiers for a product pricing slide.

put.description: Sets product pricing tiers for a slide. The slide type is automatically set to `product_pricing_v1`.

delete.description: Removes product data and resets the slide type to `standard`.

get.description: Returns all notification subscriptions for the organization.

post.description: Subscribes to an event type via a delivery channel (webhook, email, or console).

channelConfig.description: Channel-specific config. Webhook: `{ url }`. Email: `{ toEmails: ["a@b.com"] }`.

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable log of all events for the organization.

post.description: Triggers lazy generation when a viewer clicks the CTA on the intro page. Safe to call multiple times — duplicate generation requests are ignored.

get.description: Returns all product tier selections the viewer has made during the presentation.

200.description: Product selections keyed by slide ID

put.description: Saves a product tier selection for a specific slide during the presentation.

get.description: Returns the contract template, company branding, and product data needed to render the signing view.

parameters.name: configId
parameters.description: Action config to use (uses published config if omitted)

post.description: Submits a contract signature. Validates the typed signature against the customer name, resolves the template with variables, and creates an audit-trailed signed document.

signature.description: Typed signature (must match the customer name)

400.description: Bad request (signature mismatch or validation error)

post.description: Ingests interaction events from the presentation viewer (slide views, CTA clicks, Q&A logs). Authenticated via an HMAC token in the request body.
Validates a physical address using geocoding. Returns formatted address, coordinates, and normalized components.

get.description: Returns a list of available Google Fonts for use in deck branding. Results are cached for 24 hours.

post.description: Generates TTS audio for a single slide script. Requires an authenticated session. Use `/api/narration/single` for session-based caching or `/api/narration/batch` for multiple slides.
Generates TTS audio for Q&A transition or resume prompts. Uses the deck's configured voice and prompt settings.
Returns the configured resume prompt text for a deck, along with pre-generated audio if available. Falls back to a default prompt when no deck-specific settings exist.

post.summary: Analyze a slide image and generate content
post.description: Uses AI vision to analyze a slide image and generate a narration script with key points. Optionally uses deck context and slide-specific context to improve the output.

imageUrl.description: URL of the slide image to analyze

slideId.description: Slide ID (used for intro slide detection)

post.description: Generates preview audio for all slides in a deck that have narration scripts but no audio yet. Useful for previewing a deck before sending presentations.

generatedCount.description: Number of slides for which preview audio was generated

get.description: Returns the current preview audio generation status for a deck, including how many slides need preview generation.

post.description: Exchanges an existing presentation token (which may be expired up to 7 days) for a fresh HMAC token. Used by the presentation viewer to maintain event tracking authentication. Rate-limited to 10 requests per minute per presentation.

_token.description: Existing presentation token (may be expired up to 7 days)

expiresAt.description: Unix timestamp when the token expires

get.description: Returns available appointment time slots grouped by date, along with any existing appointment for the presentation. Authenticated via a presentation token. Rate-limited to 30 requests per minute.

post.description: Books an appointment for a presentation viewer. Validates the time slot is still available and creates the appointment. Rate-limited to 10 requests per minute.

409.description: Conflict (slot already booked or appointment already exists)

get.summary: Get a presentation by deck-presentation ID
get.description: Returns the full presentation data including slides, branding, FAQs, and Q&A settings. This is the primary endpoint used by the presentation viewer to load all content. No authentication required — the deck-presentation ID serves as the access token.

parameters.name: deckPresentationId
parameters.description: The deck-presentation ID from the shareable URL

410.description: Presentation no longer available (deck was deleted)

post.description: Initiates an automated roof measurement request for a homeowner address. The address is validated before submission. Poll `/api/roof-measurements/{requestId}/status` for results.

get.description: Returns the current status and results of a roof measurement request. Poll this endpoint until the status reaches `completed`, `failed`, `timed_out`, or `cancelled`.

post.description: Cancels or skips an in-progress roof measurement request. Use `cancel` to abort entirely or `skip` to mark it as skipped and proceed without measurements.

Uses AI (with regex fallback) to classify a viewer's natural-language response during a Q&A pause. Returns whether the viewer wants to continue the presentation, go back to the previous slide, or ask another question.

When the viewer was navigated to a different slide to answer a question, the classification includes a `go_back` option so they can return to where they were.

userResponse.description: The viewer's spoken or typed response

promptText.description: The prompt that was shown to the viewer (e.g. "Would you like to continue?")

didNavigateToNewSlide.description: Whether the viewer was navigated to a different slide to answer their question

currentSlideTitle.description: Title of the slide currently displayed (used for context when classifying)

intent.description: `continue` — resume the presentation from the current slide. `go_back` — return to the slide before the Q&A detour (only when `didNavigateToNewSlide` is true). `question` — the viewer has a new question.

post.summary: Detect a voice or text command
post.description: Parses natural-language input (from voice or text) to detect presentation control commands such as navigation, audio control, and CTA actions. Rate-limited to 10 requests per minute per IP and presentation.

action.description: Specific action (e.g. next, pause, open_cta, resume_here)
